---
title: "Reference"
---

This page is the factual lookup table for the deterministic layer: engine functions,
hook contracts, the workspace layout, the Theorem Ledger's 14 fields, the 6 error
categories, and the curriculum-pack YAML schemas. Signatures and field names are
transcribed verbatim from source â€” `engine/state.py`, `engine/scheduler.py`,
`engine/ledger.py`, `hooks/hooks.json` and its three scripts,
`curriculum/real-analysis/ledger-schema.yaml`, and `tests/test_curriculum.py`. Prose
here is deliberately thin; treat the tables as the source of truth and the linked file
as the source of the source.

## Engine API

The three engine modules are the deterministic layer: no model judgment, stdlib only
(`json`, `os`, `datetime`/`date`, `pathlib`, `collections.Counter` â€” each module's own
docstring says so), atomic writes throughout (`os.replace` after a `.tmp` write).

### `engine/state.py` â€” workspace discovery & learner state

Module constant: `MARKER = ".ra-tutor-workspace"`.

| Function | Behavior | Error behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `find_workspace(start: Path) -> Path \| None` | Walks `start` and its ancestors for a directory containing `MARKER`; if none is found, falls back to reading the `"workspace"` key from `~/.claude/real-analysis-tutor.json` and returns that path if its marker exists. | Never raises â€” returns `None` on any failure or if nothing is found. |
| `default_state() -> dict` | Returns the fresh-learner state dict: `{"schema_version": 1, "position": {"phase": "prelim", "chapter": None, "rung": 1}, "concepts": {}, "stuck": {}, "misconception_log": [], "session_count": 0, "profile": None}`. | Pure; never raises. |
| `load_state(workspace: Path) -> dict` | Reads `<workspace>/learner.json`; returns `default_state()` if the file does not exist. | On `JSONDecodeError`, `OSError`, or `UnicodeDecodeError`, renames the bad file to `learner.json.corrupt-<UTC timestamp>` (best-effort; a failed rename is silently swallowed) and returns `default_state()`. Never raises. |
| `save_state(workspace: Path, state: dict) -> None` | Writes `state` as JSON to `<workspace>/learner.json.tmp`, then `os.replace`s it onto `<workspace>/learner.json` (atomic). | Not caught â€” an `OSError` during the write/replace propagates to the caller. |
| `state_digest(state: dict, due: list[str]) -> str` | Builds a short digest for hook injection: position line, sessions-completed line, up to 3 weakest concepts by mastery (ascending), a due-for-review line if `due` is non-empty. Returns at most the first 6 lines joined with `\n`. | Pure; tolerant of missing keys via `.get()`; never raises. |

### `engine/scheduler.py` â€” SM-2 spaced repetition

Module docstring: "Interval math ALWAYS lives here â€” never delegated to the model."
Module constants: `REVIEWS_FILE = "reviews.json"`, `DEFAULT_EF = 2.5`, `EF_FLOOR = 1.3`.

| Function | Behavior | Error behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `record_review(reviews: list, concept: str, grade: int, today: datetime.date) -> list` | Applies SM-2 to `concept` for `grade` (0â€“5) as of `today`; returns a **new** list (input items are not mutated in place â€” matching items are replaced, new ones appended). `grade < 3` resets `reps=0, interval=1`; else `reps` increments and `interval` is 1 (first rep), 6 (second rep), or `round(interval * old_ef)` (subsequent reps). New ease factor is `max(EF_FLOOR, old_ef + 0.1 - (5-grade)*(0.08+(5-grade)*0.02))`. | Raises `ValueError(f"grade must be an int 0-5, got {grade!r}")` if `grade` is not an `int` (booleans excluded) or is outside `0â€“5`. |
| `due(reviews: list, today: datetime.date) -> list` | Returns concept names whose `due` date is on or before `today`, oldest first. | Not caught â€” `date.fromisoformat` raises `ValueError` on a malformed `"due"` string; a missing `"due"` key raises `KeyError`. |
| `load_reviews(workspace: Path) -> list` | Reads `<workspace>/reviews.json`; returns `[]` if the file does not exist. | On `JSONDecodeError` or `OSError`, returns `[]`. Never raises. |
| `save_reviews(workspace: Path, reviews: list) -> None` | Writes `reviews` as JSON to `<workspace>/reviews.json.tmp`, then `os.replace`s it onto `<workspace>/reviews.json` (atomic). | Not caught â€” an `OSError` propagates. |

### `engine/ledger.py` â€” Theorem Ledger & Error Log

Module docstring: two append-only JSON logs in the workspace â€” `ledger.json` (theorem
entries, see [Theorem Ledger field reference](#theorem-ledger-field-reference)) and
`error_log.json` (error entries, see [Error categories](#error-categories)). Both are
written atomically (temp file + `os.replace`).

Module constants: `LEDGER_FIELDS` (the 14 names, list order is the contract â€”
`tests/test_curriculum.py::test_ledger_schema_matches_engine` asserts
`curriculum/real-analysis/ledger-schema.yaml`'s `fields` list matches it exactly) and
`ERROR_CATEGORIES` (the 6 names, same order contract via `error_categories`).

| Function | Behavior | Error behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `load_ledger(workspace: Path) -> list` | Returns the list of theorem-ledger entries for `workspace` (reads `<workspace>/ledger.json`). | Returns `[]` if the file is missing, contains invalid JSON, or the parsed JSON is not a list. `OSError` on an existing-but-unreadable file is not caught. |
| `append_theorem(workspace: Path, entry: dict) -> None` | Validates `entry` has every `LEDGER_FIELDS` key, then stamps it with an `"added"` ISO date (`date.today().isoformat()`) and appends it atomically to `ledger.json`. | Raises `ValueError(f"Missing required ledger field(s): {', '.join(missing)}")` naming every missing field. |
| `append_error(workspace: Path, category: str, description: str, context: str = "") -> None` | Validates `category` is one of `ERROR_CATEGORIES`, builds a record `{"date": <UTC ISO now>, "category": category, "description": description, "context": context}`, and appends it atomically to `error_log.json`. | Raises `ValueError(f"Invalid error category: {category!r}; must be one of {ERROR_CATEGORIES}")`. |
| `error_counts(workspace: Path, last_n: int = 20) -> dict` | Returns a `dict` of category â†’ count over the most recent `last_n` entries of `error_log.json` (all entries if `last_n` is falsy). | Not caught â€” a malformed entry missing `"category"` raises `KeyError`. |

## Hook Contracts

Three hooks are registered in `hooks/hooks.json`, each run as
`python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/<script>.py"`:

| Event | Script | Registered command |
|---|---|---|
| `SessionStart` | `hooks/scripts/session_start.py` | `python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/session_start.py"` |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | `hooks/scripts/prompt_guard.py` | `python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/prompt_guard.py"` |
| `Stop` | `hooks/scripts/session_end.py` | `python3 "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/session_end.py"` |

All three carry the same three guarantees (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-real-analysis-tutor.md`):

- **Runtime budget: < 1 second.**
- **Stdlib only** â€” no third-party imports at runtime (no `yaml` in hooks); each script
  inserts the repo root onto `sys.path` (`Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]`) to
  import `engine`.
- **Fail-open** â€” every script wraps its body in `try/except Exception` and exits 0
  with no (or minimal) output on internal error, so a harness or engine bug never
  blocks the session.

### `SessionStart` â€” `hooks/scripts/session_start.py`

- **Input fields consumed from stdin JSON:** `cwd` (falls back to `Path.cwd()` if
  absent). Also calls `date.today()` directly, not from the payload.
- **Output shape (context available):**
  ```json
  {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": "<text>"}}
  ```
  printed to stdout as one line of JSON, only when `build_context()` returns non-`None`.
- **No-op cases (nothing printed, exit 0 implicitly):** unparseable/non-JSON stdin;
  stdin JSON that is not an object; `engine.state`/`engine.scheduler` failed to import
  (broken/partial install).
- **`build_context(cwd, today)` behavior** (pure function, the testable core):
  - no workspace found â†’ returns `NO_WORKSPACE_DIRECTIVE` (a conditional "offer
    onboarding only if the learner's intent warrants it" instruction).
  - workspace found but no `learner.json` yet â†’ returns `FIRST_RUN_DIRECTIVE`
    (introduce yourself, run onboarding, volunteer workspace creation and the
    never-hand-over-proofs rule).
  - returning learner â†’ returns the open-ritual directive plus `state.state_digest()`
    output plus, if the current phase has a named misconception (the
    `MISCONCEPTIONS` dict keyed `phase1`â€“`phase7`), a `"Current phase: <phase> (named
    misconception: <id>)"` line, else `"Current phase: <phase>"`.
- **Fail-open mechanism:** `main()` wraps everything in `try/except Exception`; on
  exception it best-effort logs `f"session_start.py: {exc!r}\n"` to
  `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/errors.log` if `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` is set (silently skipped
  if not, or if logging itself fails), then returns normally (exit 0).

### `UserPromptSubmit` â€” `hooks/scripts/prompt_guard.py`

- **Input fields consumed from stdin JSON:** `prompt` (default `""`), `cwd` (falls
  back to `str(Path.cwd())`).
- **Output shape (workspace active):**
  ```json
  {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit", "additionalContext": "<reminder text>"}}
  ```
- **No-op case:** no workspace found (`state.find_workspace(Path(cwd))` is `None`) â€”
  nothing printed.
- **`tier(prompt) -> int`** (pure): returns `2` if the prompt matches the `TIER2_PATTERNS`
  regex (answer-fishing, salami-slicing "first N steps", persona overrides, or the
  `AUTHORITY_PATTERNS` â€” "my professor/teacher/prof said", "the textbook/book/notes
  says", "I'm allowed"); else `1`.
- **`reminder(prompt) -> str`** (pure): tier 1 â†’ `TIER1_TEXT` (Socratic-contract
  standing reminder: no next step or complete proof, one question per turn, silent
  pre-plan before replying). Tier 2 â†’ `TIER2_BASE_TEXT` plus a tail that branches on
  whether the match was an authority claim (`TIER2_AUTHORITY_TAIL`: turn it into a
  joint verification question) or any other tier-2 trigger (`TIER2_FISHING_TAIL`:
  offer the stuck-state protocol).
- **Fail-open mechanism:** `main()` wraps the entire body in a bare
  `try/except Exception: pass` â€” any error, including a JSON parse failure on stdin,
  produces no output and no crash.

### `Stop` â€” `hooks/scripts/session_end.py`

- **Input fields consumed from stdin JSON:** `cwd` (falls back to `str(Path.cwd())`),
  `stop_hook_active` (default `False`), `session_id`.
- **Output shape (reminder due):**
  ```json
  {"decision": "block", "reason": "<REASON text>"}
  ```
  printed once, then `sys.exit(0)` regardless.
- **No-op case:** nothing printed (still `sys.exit(0)`) whenever `should_remind()` is
  `False`.
- **`should_remind(ws, now_ts, stop_hook_active, session_id) -> bool`** (pure): `False`
  if `ws is None`; `False` if `stop_hook_active` is truthy; `False` if
  `<ws>/learner.json` does not exist; `False` if the session was already reminded
  (sentinel check below); else `True` iff `now_ts - learner.json mtime > STALE_SECONDS`
  (`30 * 60`, i.e. 30 minutes).
- **Once-per-session sentinel:** file `<workspace>/.stop-reminder-session` (constant
  `SENTINEL`). Session key is `str(session_id)` if truthy, else
  `"day-" + date.today().isoformat()` (so a missing session id still caps the reminder
  at once per calendar day rather than never or every turn). `_already_reminded()`
  reads the sentinel and compares to the current session key; on a reminder,
  `record_reminded()` overwrites the sentinel with the current session key. Both
  sentinel operations swallow `OSError`.
- **Fail-open mechanism:** `main()` wraps its body in `try/except Exception: pass`,
  then unconditionally calls `sys.exit(0)` â€” the Stop hook can never itself force a
  block via an internal error.

## Workspace Layout

A workspace is any directory containing the marker file `.ra-tutor-workspace`
(`engine.state.MARKER`), discoverable from anywhere via
`engine.state.find_workspace()`. `templates/workspace/` is the copy source for a new
workspace (`skills/tutor/reference/onboarding.md`, Step 4); the remaining files are
created at onboarding time or written at runtime by the engine.

| Path | Origin | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.ra-tutor-workspace` | Copied from `templates/workspace/.ra-tutor-workspace` | Marker file; contents `{"schema_version": 1, "curriculum": "real-analysis"}`. Its presence (not its contents) is what `find_workspace()` tests for. |
| `notes/` | Copied from `templates/workspace/notes/` (ships only `.gitkeep`) | Learner notes; empty at creation. |
| `scratch/` | Copied from `templates/workspace/scratch/` (ships `lookup.lean`, a Mathlib `exact?` lookup scratch file) | Scratch Lean/working files. |
| `sessions/` | Copied from `templates/workspace/sessions/` (ships only `.gitkeep`) | Session records; empty at creation. |
| `src/` | Copied from `templates/workspace/src/` (ships only `.gitkeep`) | Lean project source; empty at creation. |
| `learner.json` | Written by `engine.state.save_state()`; first written during onboarding via `state.default_state()` with `profile`/`position.phase` set | Learner state: schema_version, position, concepts, stuck, misconception_log, session_count, profile â€” see `default_state()` above. |
| `learner.json.tmp` | `engine.state.save_state()` (transient) | Atomic-write staging file, `os.replace`d onto `learner.json`. |
| `learner.json.corrupt-<UTC timestamp>` | `engine.state.load_state()`, on a corrupt `learner.json` | Renamed-aside corrupt file; the engine then proceeds with `default_state()`. |
| `ledger.json` | Written by `engine.ledger.append_theorem()`; first created empty (`"[]"`) during onboarding | Theorem Ledger entries â€” list of dicts with the 14 `LEDGER_FIELDS` plus a stamped `"added"` date. |
| `error_log.json` | Written by `engine.ledger.append_error()`; first created empty (`"[]"`) during onboarding | Error-log entries â€” list of `{date, category, description, context}` dicts. |
| `reviews.json` | Written by `engine.scheduler.save_reviews()`; first created empty (`"[]"`) during onboarding | SM-2 review items â€” list of `{concept, ef, reps, interval, due, last_grade}` dicts. |
| `*.tmp` (`ledger.json.tmp`, `error_log.json.tmp`, `reviews.json.tmp`) | Transient, per atomic-write helper in each engine module | Staging files for `os.replace`; should never persist except mid-crash. |
| `.stop-reminder-session` | Written by `hooks/scripts/session_end.py`'s `record_reminded()` | Once-per-session sentinel for the Stop-hook persistence reminder (see Hook Contracts above). |

### The `~/.claude/real-analysis-tutor.json` pointer

Written once, at onboarding Step 4 (`skills/tutor/reference/onboarding.md`), immediately
after the workspace is created:

```json
{"workspace": "<absolute chosen path>"}
```

This is the fallback `engine.state.find_workspace()` reads when neither the current
directory nor any of its ancestors contains `.ra-tutor-workspace` â€” it is what lets
every hook and engine call work from any directory in later sessions, not only from
inside the workspace itself. `find_workspace()` still re-checks that
`<workspace>/.ra-tutor-workspace` exists before trusting the pointer; a missing or
unparseable config file, or a stale pointer to a workspace whose marker is gone,
falls through to `None` rather than raising.

## Theorem Ledger â€” Field Reference

The 14 fields of `engine.ledger.LEDGER_FIELDS`, in order. Descriptions are transcribed
from `curriculum/real-analysis/ledger-schema.yaml`, whose header states they are
"taken from the Setup Workflow Guide V2.1, Part X". Order and names are asserted
identical to `LEDGER_FIELDS` by `tests/test_curriculum.py::test_ledger_schema_matches_engine`.

| # | Field | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `theorem` | Short name (e.g., heine_cantor) |
| 2 | `statement` | In your own words â€” no copying from Rudin |
| 3 | `proof_idea` | One sentence: the essential move the proof turns on |
| 4 | `load_bearing_hypothesis` | Which hypothesis is essential? |
| 5 | `counterexample_if_removed` | Counterexample when that hypothesis is removed |
| 6 | `converse_true` | true / false / partial |
| 7 | `converse_example` | Canonical counterexample if converse is false |
| 8 | `misconception` | The named misconception for this theorem, drawn from the Part V catalogue |
| 9 | `misconception_refutation` | The precise logical step that is invalid, plus the canonical counterexample that breaks the misconception |
| 10 | `depends_on` | List of earlier Ledger entries this proof invokes |
| 11 | `downstream` | Which later results depend on this theorem |
| 12 | `analogues` | Which earlier theorem has a similar logical form or proof strategy |
| 13 | `lean_status` | compiled / in_progress / planned / unverified |
| 14 | `bloom_level` | highest level achieved: apply / analyze / evaluate / create |

`engine.ledger.append_theorem()` additionally stamps every entry with `added`
(`date.today().isoformat()`) â€” a 15th key present on disk but not part of the
`LEDGER_FIELDS` validation contract or the schema's `fields` list.

## Error Categories

The 6 categories of `engine.ledger.ERROR_CATEGORIES`, in order. Descriptions are
transcribed from `curriculum/real-analysis/ledger-schema.yaml`, whose header states
they are "taken from the RealAnalysis CompleteGuide V2.1, Part IX, 'Error Log' table."

| # | Category | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `quantifier_error` | Wrong order of quantifiers; swapping for-all and there-exists |
| 2 | `missing_hypothesis` | Applying a theorem without verifying all its conditions |
| 3 | `invalid_inference` | A step that does not follow logically from what precedes it |
| 4 | `wrong_theorem` | Citing a theorem whose conclusion does not match what is needed |
| 5 | `delta_depends_on_x` | Choosing delta that depends on the point x in a uniform continuity proof |
| 6 | `converse_confusion` | Assuming the converse of a conditional theorem |

## Curriculum Pack Schema

Required structure per curriculum file, as enforced by `tests/test_curriculum.py`
against `curriculum/real-analysis/`. Every file listed here is required
(`REQUIRE_ALL = True` â€” a missing file fails the corresponding test rather than
skipping it).

| File | Top-level key(s) | Per-item required keys | Additional constraints (test) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ledger-schema.yaml` | `fields` (list), `error_categories` (list) | each item: `name` | `[f["name"] for f in fields]` must equal `list(ledger.LEDGER_FIELDS)` exactly (order matters); `[c["name"] for c in error_categories]` must equal `list(ledger.ERROR_CATEGORIES)` exactly (order matters). |
| `misconceptions.yaml` | `misconceptions` (list) | `id`, `statement`, `corrupted_step`, `refutation`, `counterexample`, `phase`, `topic` (all truthy) | The set of `id`s must equal exactly: `completeness-implies-convergence`, `compact-iff-closed-and-bounded`, `consecutive-closeness-implies-convergence`, `pencil-lifting-image`, `continuity-implies-uniform-continuity`, `terms-to-zero-implies-convergence`, `pointwise-limit-preserves-continuity`. |
| `curriculum.yaml` | `phases` (list) | each phase: `texts`, `weeks`, `name` (all truthy) | Phase `id`s, in order, must equal `["prelim", "phase1", "phase2", ..., "phase9"]`. `phase4`'s `misconception` must equal `"pencil-lifting-image"`; `phase8`, `phase9`, and `prelim`'s `misconception` must be `None`. |
| `checklists/phase{1..7}.yaml` (7 files) | â€” (flat document) | `phase`, `topic`, `items`, `converse_checks`, `lean_targets`, `rudin_exercises`, `misconception` (all `is not None`) | `len(items) >= 5`. |
| `ladder.yaml` | `rungs` (list) | each rung: `name`, `claude_role`, `bloom_levels`, `problem_classes` (all truthy) | Rung numbers, in order, must equal `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`. |

::: {.callout-note}
The misconception entries require seven keys in total: `id` is enforced by the id-set assertion in `tests/test_curriculum.py`, and the remaining six (`statement`, `corrupted_step`, `refutation`, `counterexample`, `phase`, `topic`) by the per-field truthy check.
:::
